convergence.recommended_position: KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: Under congenial proper MI with an estimated scale and the complete-data MLE target, the averaged log-likelihood across imputations overstates the complete-data log-likelihood by +1/2 tr(RIV) plus a MAR design-imbalance term that vanishes exactly under MCAR; the proper-MI proof and the MAR design-imbalance term are the novel parts, while the trace magnitude coincides with Shimodaira's AIC_{x;y} penalty. The revision fixes novelty scoping; real, applied importance, and technical scope otherwise pass.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: Under the paper's congenial proper-MI conditions and on the usual -2 log-likelihood criterion scale, applied MI AIC/BIC tables targeting complete-data-equivalent selection should add a per-candidate tr(RIV_k) correction to each criterion, because uncorrected criteria tilt toward models with more missing information. The revision fixes correct scope by making assumptions and sign/scale explicit; real, novelty, and applied importance pass.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: For information-criterion ranking, where no calibrated reference distribution absorbs the mean, use the per-candidate tr(RIV_k) centering correction for complete-data-equivalent selection; for a Chan-style calibrated nested likelihood-ratio test, do not add a separate numerator mean correction because the null mean is already absorbed by the reference distribution. The revision fixes scope by limiting the no-double-count warning to calibrated nested LRTs and by avoiding an overstrong must-restore-ranking reading.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: The MI information-criterion bias is model-specific: candidate models can have different RIV traces, so on the usual -2 log-likelihood criterion scale adding tr(RIV_k) supplies the theoretical centering correction for MI-AIC and MI-BIC under the complete-data-equivalent target. The revision fixes scale and target scope; other criteria pass.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: We treat the structured AI-human workflow as a first-class methodological contribution by documenting, for this study, ORCID-verified human provenance with mechanically checked records and auditable gates for sourced citations, human acceptance of every result, dual computer-algebra verification, preregistered simulations with reported failures, adversarial rederivation, and full reproducibility, while marking same-lineage qualitative session-coding frequencies as model judgments rather than independently human-coded facts. The revision fixes correct scope and novelty framing by carrying the no-human-second-coder limit; real and applied importance pass.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: For nested MI likelihood-ratio comparisons at the null, the numerator's differential deviance bias is tr(RIV_perp), the missing information in the tested directions projected in the complete-data information metric, not tr(RIV_full) minus tr(RIV_0). The revision fixes wording and deviance-scale precision; real, novelty, applied importance, and scope pass.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: Applied teams evaluating AI-assisted statistical derivations should ask for commensurate auditable evidence before relying on the results: sourced citations, independent symbolic checks where applicable, preregistered numerical criteria with reported failures, adversarial review, and reproducibility records. The revision fixes scope by making the checklist a reliance standard rather than a universal proof of correctness; other criteria pass.

KEEP-WITH-REVISION. Exact revised sentence: For nested MI likelihood-ratio comparisons at the null or local alternatives, fit the competing models to the same imputed data sets whenever possible, because this pairing reduces Monte Carlo noise from order sqrt(n) to order one. The revision fixes scope by adding the null/local-alternative condition and whenever possible; real, novelty, and applied importance pass.

CONSOLIDATE TO TWO. Introduction contributions list: For information-criterion selection after proper MI, the deviance bias is model-specific: candidate models can have different RIV traces, so uncorrected MI-AIC/BIC tilt toward higher-missing-information candidates; on the usual -2 log-likelihood scale, adding tr(RIV_k) to each criterion supplies the centering correction for complete-data-equivalent selection. Discussion applied-implications passage: In practice, use the per-candidate tr(RIV_k) correction for IC ranking when the target is complete-data-equivalent selection, because no reference distribution absorbs the mean; do not add a separate numerator mean correction to a Chan-style calibrated nested likelihood-ratio test, where the null mean is already built into the reference distribution. Do not keep items 2, 3, and 4 as three separate skim bullets.

COMPLETE. The eight-item set is sufficient; the W3-C=1.000 result, calibration ladder, and Shimodaira equivalence should be featured as supporting evidence, lineage, and boundary cases inside the IC section and Discussion, but making any of them a separate skim-layer contribution would dilute the action message because each validates or contextualizes the already-ratified IC correction rather than adding a separate applied action.

Lead with item 2. Exact abstract hook: MI AIC/BIC tables built on multiply-imputed data are systematically tilted toward candidate models with more missing information; under congenial proper MI, we prove the model-specific trace bias and show that adding the per-candidate correction tr(RIV_k) to the usual -2 log-likelihood criteria recenters them for complete-data-equivalent model selection.

FIRST-CLASS. Exact one-sentence framing: We treat the AI-human workflow as a first-class methodological contribution, co-equal with the technical results, because this study provides mechanically verified provenance and verification records--sourced citations, human acceptance gates, dual symbolic checks, preregistered simulations, adversarial rederivation, reproducibility artifacts, turn counts, dated episodes, and the 8-of-9 sign-flip catch--while reporting same-lineage session-coding frequencies as model judgments rather than independently human-coded measurements.
convergence.residual_dissent: Minority concern remains that the absence of a human second coder could justify ranking the workflow as supporting rather than co-equal; the first-class framing should be ratified only if the caveat is carried prominently.
